core_mechanism: a deterministic, order-independent (abelian) local mass-balancing relaxation rule that redistributes excess from overloaded sites to neighbours until stable, acting as a derandomized surrogate for a diffusion/random-walk aggregation process and driving the aggregate to an isotropic (Euclidean-ball) scaling limit
brief_reason: The abstract frames the rotor-router as a deterministic analogue of random walk and the divisible sandpile as excess-mass redistribution, then proves the occupied region is asymptotically a Euclidean ball, i.e. a local order-independent relaxation rule reproducing diffusion's isotropic aggregate shape.
